draft.introduction: Surah Al-Imran (The Family of Imran) addresses the relationship between Islam and Christianity, narrating the births of Mary and Jesus while asserting strict monotheism. It was partly revealed in response to a Christian delegation from Najran.

translation.text: Alif. Lam. Mim. God—there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of existence. He has sent down upon you the Book in truth, confirming what was before it. And He revealed the Torah and the Gospel before, as guidance for the people. It is He who has sent down to you the Book; in it are verses precise—they are the foundation of the Book—and others unspecific.
translation.original_text: الم اللَّهُ لَا إِلَـهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ

translator_notes.note: The distinction between “precise” (muhkamat) and “unspecific” (mutashabihat) verses is foundational to Quranic hermeneutics. It acknowledges interpretive ambiguity within the text itself and has generated centuries of debate about which verses are allegorical and which literal.
